concepts.title: Definition of Health
concepts.description: Health is a state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity.

concepts.title: Types of Diseases
concepts.description: Diseases can be classified into Infectious and Non-Infectious, Acute and Chronic, and Communicable and Non-Communicable.

concepts.title: Causes of Diseases
concepts.description: Diseases can be caused by Genetic, Environmental, and Lifestyle factors.

concepts.title: Pathogens
concepts.description: Microorganisms (Bacteria, Virus, Fungus) are the causative agents of disease and can be remembered using the mnemonic 'BVF'.

concepts.title: Epidemiology
concepts.description: Students often confuse the terms 'Epidemic' and 'Pandemic'. An Epidemic is a widespread outbreak of disease, while a Pandemic is a global outbreak of disease.

concepts.title: WHO Definition of Health
concepts.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) defines health as 'a state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity'.

concepts.title: Types of Inheritance
concepts.description: Inheritance can be classified into Autosomal Dominant, Autosomal Recessive, X-linked Dominant, and X-linked Recessive patterns.

concepts.title: Health-Seeking Behaviour
concepts.description: Health-seeking behaviour is influenced by factors such as awareness, accessibility, affordability, and acceptability of healthcare services.
